description:
## The change

Make `nil`, `true`, `false` regular atoms with reserved compile-time
atom IDs. `NIL_BITS`/`TRUE_BITS`/`FALSE_BITS` constants stay as named
references; their definitions shift from `TAG_SPECIAL`-tagged to
`TAG_ATOM`-tagged.

### Reserve

  AtomTable interns "nil" (id=0), "true" (id=1), "false" (id=2) at
  construction time, before any user-source atoms. Existing
  `match_error`/`function_clause` reservations slide to ids 3/4.
  Document the contract in `src/atoms.rs` (or wherever AtomTable
  lives).

### Redefine

  runtime/src/fz_value.rs:
    Remove: SPECIAL_NIL, SPECIAL_TRUE, SPECIAL_FALSE.
    Remove: TAG_SPECIAL tag class (becomes empty/reserved).
    Add:    NIL_ATOM_ID = 0, TRUE_ATOM_ID = 1, FALSE_ATOM_ID = 2.
    Redefine: pub const NIL_BITS / TRUE_BITS / FALSE_BITS as
              `(id << TAG_BITS) | TAG_ATOM` for the reserved IDs.
    FzValue::is_nil/is_true/is_false impls switch from
              `self.0 == SPECIAL_*` to `unbox_atom() == Some(reserved_id)`.
    Debug impl: Tag::Atom branch detects reserved IDs and prints
              `FzValue::Nil` / `FzValue::True` / `FzValue::False`
              instead of `FzValue::Atom(0/1/2)`.
    unbox_ptr: the special-bits early-return goes away (no more
              SPECIAL values to filter).

  src/ir_codegen.rs:
    NIL_BITS / TRUE_BITS / FALSE_BITS constants redefined to match.
    All call sites unchanged — they reference by name.

  src/ir_lower.rs:
    AtomTable pre-interns "nil"/"true"/"false" at construction.
    `match_error` and `function_clause` ids shift; any test asserting
    specific atom-id values (id=0 for match_error, id=1 for
    function_clause) needs updating — those tests now assert id=3
    and id=4.

  src/ir_interp.rs:
    Const::Nil/True/False eval to FzValue with atom-tagged bits via
    the new constants. is_nil/is_true/is_false in interp use the
    new impls.

### Tests / goldens

  - Tests that pin specific atom IDs (match_error == 0,
    function_clause == 1) get updated.
  - Tests that pin halt-values that came from nil/true/false
    (halt_value == 3 for nil, == 11 for true, == 19 for false) get
    updated to the new atom-tagged values
    (0 << 3 | 2 = 2 for nil, 1 << 3 | 2 = 10 for true,
     2 << 3 | 2 = 18 for false).
  - CLIF goldens re-bless (different iconst values).
  - expected.txt unchanged everywhere (the visible "nil"/"true"/"false"
    print strings are preserved by Debug impl + NA.3's pretty-print).

## Removes

  - SPECIAL_NIL, SPECIAL_TRUE, SPECIAL_FALSE constants.
  - TAG_SPECIAL tag class.
  - The "is this in TAG_SPECIAL space" filter in unbox_ptr.
  - Any direct `self.0 == SPECIAL_*` comparisons.

## Depends on

Nothing (foundation of the epic).

## Hazards

  - **Atom-id stability**: the reservation has to happen at AtomTable
    construction. Every Module's AtomTable starts with these three
    reserved. Tests that assume specific IDs for `match_error` /
    `function_clause` will break — update them.
  - **Cross-module atom IDs**: today atom IDs are CompiledModule-scoped
    (per `atom_identity_preserved_across_processes_from_same_module`).
    The reserved IDs are stable per-module; same module, same IDs.
    Cross-module comparisons aren't affected by this change.
title: fz-NA.1: runtime — reserve atom IDs, redefine *_BITS as atom-tagged
